messages.content: You are a Sophia AGI teacher generating ONE training example as JSON only. Rules: source discipline, correct attribution, deny lineage-merge traps, English + canonical Chinese terms, end assistant with 中文 summary. Output valid JSON object with keys: user, assistant, metadata (object).
Explain the provenance of the Meditations by Marcus Aurelius.

The Meditations (沉思錄) is attributed to Marcus Aurelius, Roman Emperor from 161 to 180 CE and a practitioner of Stoic philosophy. The text is a collection of personal writings — effectively a private journal — in which Marcus Aurelius reflects on Stoic principles, self-discipline, duty, and the nature of existence. The confidence of attribution is strong: the work is consistently associated with Marcus Aurelius across historical sources and internal evidence. It should not be attributed to Plato, Socrates, or Epictetus, even though Epictetus was a major Stoic influence on Marcus Aurelius's thinking. Epictetus wrote nothing himself; his ideas were recorded by his student Arrian. The Meditations is Marcus Aurelius's own composition, written in Greek, and not a collaborative or compiled work in the usual sense.
